text: {"claims":[{"statement":"The overwhelming majority of participants (98%) viewed maternal and childhood vaccines in the immunization schedule favorably due to the positive track record of eradicating diseases and preventing morbidity and deaths, especially among children.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The overwhelming majority of participants (98%) viewed maternal and childhood vaccines in the immunization schedule favorably due to the positive track record of eradicating diseases and preventing morbidity and deaths, especially among children.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly reports the proportion (98%) of participants who held a favorable view of maternal and childhood vaccines, supporting the claim about the majority's favorable perception.","population":"participants","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"favorable view of maternal and childhood vaccines","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"98%","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Nearly half of the participants had concerns about the COVID-19 vaccines, including concerns about efficacy, safety, side effects, short development period, and belief that COVID-19 is not real in Nigeria.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Nearly half of the participants had concerns about the COVID-19 vaccines. For example, community members were concerned about vaccine efficacy and safety due to reported side effects and unease about the short period of vaccine development. Other concerns raised were that the vaccines are not needed in Nigeria because COVID-19 is not real, and they had not seen an infected person.","entailment":"The quoted passage enumerates the various concerns held by roughly half of the participants, providing evidence for the claim about widespread concerns regarding COVID-19 vaccines.","population":"participants","intervention":"COVID-19 vaccines","comparator":"","outcome":"concerns about efficacy, safety, side effects, short development period, belief that COVID-19 is not real","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"nearly half","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Some health workers expressed doubt about the COVID-19 vaccine effectiveness, stating it is 50% effective and 50% not effective.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Well, we are still having a little doubt because they are saying it is 50–50, that is 50% effective and 50% not effective","entailment":"The health worker’s quoted statement directly conveys their doubt and the perceived 50% effectiveness figure, supporting the claim about health workers’ skepticism.","population":"health workers","intervention":"COVID-19 vaccine","comparator":"","outcome":"doubt about effectiveness (perceived 50% effective)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"50% effective, 50% not effective","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Exposure to unsubstantiated claims about COVID-19 was universal among the study participants.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"We found that exposure to unsubstantiated claims about COVID-19 was universal among our participants, confirming the ubiquity of these false narratives.","entailment":"The quoted sentence explicitly states that all participants were exposed to unsubstantiated claims, providing direct evidence for the claim of universal exposure.","population":"study participants","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"universal exposure to unsubstantiated COVID-19 claims","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"universal","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The leading conspiracy theory identified was that SARS CoV-2 does not exist, and the pandemic response is a means for Nigerian government officials and politicians to obtain pecuniary gains from misusing response funds.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In our study, the leading conspiracy theory was that SARS CoV-2 does not exist; therefore, the pandemic response is a means for the Nigerian government officials and politicians to obtain pecuniary gains from misusing the response funds.","entailment":"The quoted passage directly names the primary conspiracy theory and its alleged motive, supporting the claim about the content of the leading conspiracy theory.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"belief that SARS CoV-2 does not exist and pandemic response is for pecuniary gain","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"}]}
